name: Thiamine
notes: Essential for carbohydrate metabolism and nerve function

name: Riboflavin
notes: May turn urine bright yellow; important for energy metabolism

name: Riboflavin 5-Phosphate
notes: Active coenzyme form of riboflavin; better absorbed by some

name: Niacin
notes: Flush form; may cause skin flushing at higher doses

name: Niacinamide
notes: No-flush form; widely used for skin and metabolic support

name: Nicotinamide Riboside
notes: NAD+ precursor; may support mitochondrial function and healthy aging

name: NMN
notes: NAD+ precursor; popular longevity supplement; take without food for best absorption per some protocols

name: Pantothenic Acid
notes: Supports adrenal function and coenzyme A synthesis

name: Pantethine
notes: Active form of B5; used for cholesterol and lipid metabolism support

name: Pyridoxine HCl
notes: Standard B6 form; avoid very high doses long-term due to neuropathy risk

name: P5P
notes: Active coenzyme form of B6; preferred for those with B6 conversion issues

name: Biotin
notes: High-dose biotin may interfere with thyroid lab tests; pause before bloodwork

name: Folate
notes: Synthetic folic acid form; some prefer methylfolate for MTHFR variants

name: L-Methylfolate
notes: Active form preferred for MTHFR variants; bypasses conversion step

name: Folinic Acid
notes: Another active folate form; does not require MTHFR enzyme for conversion

name: Methylcobalamin
notes: Active form of B12; sublingual or injectable preferred for absorption

name: Adenosylcobalamin
notes: Mitochondrial form of B12; often combined with methylcobalamin

name: Cyanocobalamin
notes: Most common B12 form; requires conversion to active forms in body

name: Hydroxocobalamin
notes: Longer-acting B12 form; often used in injections; good for detox support

name: B Complex
notes: Comprehensive B vitamin blend; useful as daily foundation

name: Vitamin A
notes: Fat-soluble; avoid excess during pregnancy due to teratogen risk

name: Beta-Carotene
notes: Plant-derived precursor to vitamin A; safer than preformed A during pregnancy

name: Vitamin C
notes: Water-soluble antioxidant; bowel tolerance varies widely

name: Liposomal Vitamin C
notes: Enhanced bioavailability via liposomal delivery; less GI upset than standard C

name: Buffered Vitamin C
notes: Gentler on stomach than ascorbic acid; good for sensitive GI

name: Ester-C
notes: Patented form with calcium threonate; marketed for sustained release

name: Vitamin D3
notes: Check serum 25-OH-D levels to calibrate dose; optimal range 40-60 ng/mL

name: Vitamin D3 + K2
notes: K2 directs calcium to bones rather than arteries; important co-factor with D3

name: Vitamin E Mixed Tocopherols
notes: Prefer mixed tocopherols over isolated alpha; avoid synthetic dl-alpha-tocopherol

name: Tocotrienols
notes: Less common E family; research interest in cardiovascular and neuroprotective effects

name: Vitamin K1
notes: Important for blood clotting; check interactions with warfarin

name: Vitamin K2 MK-4
notes: Short-acting K2; requires multiple daily doses; bone and cardiovascular support

name: Vitamin K2 MK-7
notes: Longer-acting K2 from fermentation; single daily dose sufficient

name: Magnesium Glycinate
intakeConditions: can take with or without food
notes: Preferred form for sleep and muscle relaxation; well tolerated

name: Magnesium Malate
notes: Good for energy and muscle pain; malic acid supports Krebs cycle

name: Magnesium Threonate
notes: Crosses blood-brain barrier; used for cognitive support and sleep

name: Magnesium Citrate
notes: Good bioavailability; mild laxative effect at higher doses

name: Magnesium L-Threonate
notes: Patented form for brain magnesium levels; supports memory and cognition

name: Magnesium Oxide
notes: Poorest absorbed form; mainly used as laxative or cheap filler in supplements

name: Magnesium Taurate
notes: Taurine + magnesium combination; studied for cardiovascular and nervous system support

name: Zinc Picolinate
intakeConditions: away from food or with small meal
notes: Well-absorbed form; take separate from iron and calcium; can cause nausea on empty stomach

name: Zinc Glycinate
notes: Chelated form with good bioavailability and tolerability

name: Zinc Bisglycinate
notes: Amino acid chelate; minimal GI side effects

name: Zinc Acetate
intakeConditions: dissolve in mouth; do not swallow whole
notes: Used for cold symptom reduction; start within 24 hours of symptoms

name: Calcium Citrate
notes: Better absorbed than carbonate, especially for low stomach acid; do not exceed 500mg per dose

name: Calcium Carbonate
intakeConditions: with food (requires stomach acid for absorption)
notes: Requires stomach acid; antacid at higher doses; inexpensive

name: Iron Bisglycinate
intakeConditions: on empty stomach or with vitamin C
notes: Gentlest form of iron; minimal constipation; well absorbed

name: Iron Fumarate
intakeConditions: with vitamin C; away from calcium and coffee
notes: High elemental iron content; may cause GI side effects

name: Iron Sulfate
intakeConditions: with vitamin C; away from calcium and coffee
notes: Most common pharmaceutical iron; frequent GI side effects

name: Selenium
notes: Important for thyroid function; do not exceed 400mcg/day; toxicity risk at high doses

name: Chromium Picolinate
notes: Supports insulin sensitivity and glucose metabolism

name: Boron
notes: Supports bone health, testosterone, and vitamin D metabolism

name: Manganese
notes: Required for bone formation and antioxidant enzymes; avoid excess

name: Molybdenum
notes: Cofactor for sulfite oxidase and other enzymes; helps with sulfite sensitivity

name: Iodine
notes: Critical for thyroid hormone synthesis; iodine and selenium should be taken together

name: Potassium
intakeConditions: with food to avoid GI upset
notes: Most supplements limited to 99mg; best obtained from diet; check with doctor if on cardiac meds

name: Copper
notes: Balance with zinc (8:1 zinc:copper ratio); important for connective tissue and immune function

name: Fish Oil
intakeConditions: with fat-containing meal to reduce fishy burps
notes: Refrigerate after opening; check IFOS certification for purity

name: Omega-3
notes: Broad term covering ALA, EPA, DHA; marine sources most bioavailable

name: EPA
notes: Anti-inflammatory omega-3; often highlighted for mood and cardiovascular support

name: DHA
notes: Critical for brain development and function; important during pregnancy and infancy

name: EPA+DHA
notes: Combined form; ideal ratio approximately 2:1 EPA:DHA for most adults

name: Algae Omega-3
notes: Vegan/vegetarian source of EPA+DHA; derived from algae (same source as fish)

name: Krill Oil
notes: Phospholipid form may be better absorbed; contains astaxanthin antioxidant; avoid if shellfish allergy

name: Flaxseed Oil
notes: Plant-based ALA; conversion to EPA/DHA is inefficient in humans

name: Evening Primrose Oil
notes: Contains GLA (gamma-linolenic acid); used for hormone balance and skin

name: Borage Oil
notes: Highest GLA content of any plant oil; often used for inflammatory conditions

name: GLA
notes: Anti-inflammatory omega-6; found in evening primrose, borage, and black currant oils

name: Cod Liver Oil
notes: Natural source of vitamins A and D plus EPA/DHA; be mindful of total vitamin A intake

name: L-Glutamine
intakeConditions: on empty stomach or between meals
notes: Supports gut lining integrity; muscle recovery; immune function

name: L-Lysine
notes: Essential amino acid; used for cold sore prevention and immune support

name: L-Arginine
notes: Nitric oxide precursor; may support circulation and exercise performance

name: L-Citrulline
notes: More effective than arginine for raising arginine/NO levels; popular in pre-workout

name: L-Tyrosine
notes: Dopamine precursor; supports focus, mood, and thyroid hormone synthesis

name: L-Theanine
notes: Promotes calm focus; often paired with caffeine; supports sleep at higher doses

name: L-Carnitine
notes: Supports fat metabolism and mitochondrial function; lower bioavailability in vegetarians

name: ALCAR
notes: Crosses blood-brain barrier; neuroprotective; energy and cognitive support

name: L-Cysteine
notes: Precursor to glutathione; antioxidant support

name: NAC
notes: Glutathione precursor; mucolytic; liver support; used in acetaminophen overdose

name: Glycine
notes: Supports sleep quality, collagen synthesis, and detoxification

name: Taurine
notes: Conditionally essential; supports heart, liver, eyes, and nervous system

name: GABA
notes: Inhibitory neurotransmitter; may support relaxation and sleep; CNS penetration debated

name: 5-HTP
notes: Serotonin precursor; do not combine with SSRIs; supports mood and sleep

name: L-Tryptophan
intakeConditions: away from protein meals; with carbs
notes: Serotonin and melatonin precursor; competes with other amino acids at BBB

name: L-Phenylalanine
notes: Precursor to tyrosine, dopamine, and norepinephrine; avoid with phenylketonuria

name: BCAAs
notes: Leucine, isoleucine, valine; support muscle protein synthesis and reduce muscle breakdown

name: Creatine Monohydrate
notes: Most researched performance supplement; supports strength, muscle, and cognitive function

name: Beta-Alanine
notes: Causes harmless tingling (paresthesia); supports carnosine levels and exercise endurance

name: Collagen Peptides
intakeConditions: with vitamin C for best absorption
notes: Supports skin, joints, and gut; not a complete protein

name: Hydrolyzed Collagen
notes: Pre-digested collagen; mixes well in liquids; supports joint and skin health

name: Ashwagandha KSM-66
notes: Adaptogen for stress, cortisol, thyroid support; KSM-66 is standardized root extract

name: Rhodiola Rosea
intakeConditions: on empty stomach or before food
notes: Adaptogen for fatigue, stress, and mental performance; take in morning to avoid insomnia

name: Holy Basil
notes: Adaptogen for blood sugar, stress, and immune support; also available as herbal tea

name: Eleuthero
notes: Mild adaptogen; supports stamina and stress adaptation

name: Maca Root
notes: Adaptogen for energy, fertility, and hormone balance; gelatinized form easier to digest

name: Schisandra
notes: Adaptogen supporting liver health, stress resilience, and cognitive function

name: Panax Ginseng
notes: Stimulating adaptogen; may raise blood pressure; avoid near bedtime

name: American Ginseng
notes: More calming than Korean ginseng; used for immune and energy support

name: Astragalus
notes: Immune modulating adaptogen; telomere support; long-term use traditional in TCM

name: Shilajit
notes: Contains fulvic acid and trace minerals; supports energy, testosterone, and mitochondria

name: Cordyceps
notes: Adaptogenic mushroom; supports energy, VO2 max, adrenal, and immune function

name: Reishi
notes: Calming adaptogenic mushroom; liver support, immune modulation, sleep quality

name: Lion's Mane
notes: Supports NGF production, cognitive function, and nerve regeneration

name: Chaga
notes: Antioxidant-rich mushroom; immune support and anti-inflammatory

name: Turkey Tail
notes: Immune modulating mushroom; contains PSK used in cancer adjunct therapy in Japan

name: Mucuna Pruriens
notes: Contains natural L-DOPA; supports dopamine, testosterone, and mood; use with caution

name: Turmeric
notes: Potent anti-inflammatory; bioavailability greatly enhanced by piperine and fat

name: Curcumin
notes: Active constituent of turmeric; BCM-95 or phytosome form has superior bioavailability

name: BCM-95 Curcumin
notes: Proprietary curcumin-essential oil complex with 7x better bioavailability than standard curcumin

name: Ginger
notes: Anti-nausea, anti-inflammatory, digestive support; safe in pregnancy for nausea

name: Milk Thistle
notes: Liver protective; standardized to 70-80% silymarin; supports liver detoxification

name: Saw Palmetto
notes: Supports prostate health and may reduce DHT; used for BPH and hair loss

name: Echinacea
notes: Immune stimulating; best used at onset of illness; not for long-term daily use

name: Elderberry
notes: Antiviral immune support; used for cold and flu; syrup and capsule forms available

name: Berberine
notes: Activates AMPK; blood sugar and lipid support comparable to metformin; space from other medications

name: Ginkgo Biloba
notes: Supports cerebral circulation and cognitive function; may interact with blood thinners

name: Grapeseed Extract
notes: Potent antioxidant; vascular support; may complement vitamin C

name: Green Tea Extract
intakeConditions: with food; avoid on empty stomach

name: Resveratrol
notes: SIRT1 activator; longevity and cardiovascular interest; pair with quercetin for synergy

name: Quercetin
notes: Anti-inflammatory flavonoid; antiviral; senolytic properties at higher doses

name: Lutein
notes: Eye health; macular pigment support; often combined with zeaxanthin

name: Zeaxanthin
notes: Carotenoid for macular health; found with lutein in eye supplements

name: Lycopene
notes: Carotenoid antioxidant; prostate and cardiovascular support

name: Bilberry
notes: Anthocyanins support eye health, circulation, and blood sugar

name: Valerian Root
notes: Sleep support; mild sedative; may take 2-4 weeks for full effect

name: Passionflower
notes: Anxiolytic and sleep-promoting; gentle enough for day use for anxiety

name: Chamomile
notes: Gentle calming herb; sleep and GI support; also available as tea

name: Lavender
notes: Oral lavender (Silexan) clinically studied for anxiety; essential oil also used topically

name: Black Cohosh
notes: Used for menopausal symptoms; not recommended more than 6 months continuous use; liver caution

name: Dong Quai
notes: Traditional herb for menstrual and menopausal support; avoid in pregnancy and with blood thinners

name: Red Clover
notes: Phytoestrogens for menopausal support; may interact with hormone therapies

name: Vitex
notes: Supports progesterone balance and PMS/PMDD; takes 3+ months for effect

name: Tribulus Terrestris
notes: Used for libido and testosterone support; evidence mixed in clinical trials

name: Fenugreek
notes: Supports testosterone, blood sugar, and lactation; may cause maple syrup odor in sweat/urine

name: Cinnamon
intakeConditions: with meals for blood sugar support
notes: Ceylon preferred over cassia for supplemental use; blood sugar and insulin sensitivity support

name: Garlic
notes: Cardiovascular, antimicrobial, immune support; aged extract is odor-controlled

name: Cayenne
notes: Metabolism, circulation, digestive support; topical capsaicin for pain

name: Hawthorn Berry
notes: Cardiovascular tonic; supports heart function, circulation, and blood pressure

name: Boswellia
notes: Anti-inflammatory; supports joint and digestive health; standardized to AKBA preferred

name: White Willow Bark
notes: Natural salicylate; anti-inflammatory and analgesic; avoid if salicylate sensitive or on blood thinners

name: Lactobacillus acidophilus
notes: Classic probiotic strain; supports gut flora, immunity, and vaginal health

name: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG
notes: Most researched probiotic strain; diarrhea prevention, immune support, especially in children

name: Bifidobacterium longum
notes: Important for gut health and immune regulation; declines with age

name: Bifidobacterium infantis
notes: IBS support; supports gut lining integrity

name: Saccharomyces boulardii
notes: Yeast-based probiotic; not affected by antibiotics; C. diff and traveler's diarrhea support

name: Lactobacillus plantarum
notes: Robust probiotic; resilient to stomach acid; IBS and gut barrier support

name: Lactobacillus reuteri
notes: Infant colic, bone health, testosterone, and oral health support; well-studied strain

name: Lactobacillus casei
notes: Supports immune function and gut motility; found in some fermented dairy products

name: Multi-Strain Probiotic
notes: Blend of multiple Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium strains for broad gut support

name: Spore-Based Probiotic
notes: Spore-forming Bacillus strains survive stomach acid without refrigeration; shelf-stable

name: Prebiotic Fiber
notes: Feeds beneficial gut bacteria; start low and increase slowly to avoid gas/bloating

name: Inulin
notes: Prebiotic fructan; bifidobacterium food; start low to avoid GI discomfort

name: FOS
notes: Prebiotic; bifidogenic; naturally found in onions, garlic, bananas

name: GOS
notes: Prebiotic that selectively increases Bifidobacterium; found in human breast milk

name: Psyllium Husk
intakeConditions: with full glass of water (8 oz)
notes: Soluble fiber; lowers LDL cholesterol; regulates bowel movements; take with plenty of water

name: Slippery Elm
intakeConditions: with water; take separately from medications
notes: Mucilaginous herb; soothes GI mucosa; useful for IBD, reflux, and gut healing

name: DGL
intakeConditions: chew or dissolve 20 min before eating
notes: Licorice with glycyrrhizin removed; gut mucosa support; safe for blood pressure unlike regular licorice

name: Aloe Vera
notes: Gut soothing; immune support; ensure inner leaf fillet (remove aloin/anthraquinones)

name: Digestive Enzyme Blend
intakeConditions: with the first bite of food
notes: Broad spectrum enzymes for protein, fat, carb, and fiber digestion

name: Bromelain
intakeConditions: on empty stomach for anti-inflammatory; with food for digestion
notes: Proteolytic enzyme from pineapple; anti-inflammatory when taken away from food

name: Papain
notes: Proteolytic enzyme from papaya; digestive support

name: Serrapeptase
notes: Proteolytic enzyme; anti-inflammatory, scar tissue breakdown; must be taken away from food

name: Nattokinase
notes: Fibrinolytic enzyme from natto; cardiovascular and blood viscosity support; caution with blood thinners

name: Wobenzym
notes: Systemic enzyme blend with bromelain, papain, pancreatin, and rutin; anti-inflammatory

name: Betaine HCl
notes: Supports stomach acid production; start low and increase; stop if burning sensation

name: Ox Bile
notes: Supports fat digestion; useful post-cholecystectomy or with bile insufficiency

name: Pancreatin
notes: Contains lipase, amylase, protease from porcine pancreas; supports fat and carb digestion

name: DHEA
notes: Adrenal hormone precursor; levels tested before supplementing; may convert to estrogen or testosterone

name: Pregnenolone
notes: Precursor to all steroid hormones; used with hormonal testing guidance

name: Melatonin 0.5mg
notes: Lowest effective dose for most; circadian reset; start here before using higher doses

name: Melatonin 1mg
notes: Common low dose for sleep onset support

name: Melatonin 3mg
notes: Common dose sold OTC; may be excessive for sensitive individuals

name: Melatonin 5mg
notes: Higher OTC dose; may cause grogginess; extended-release form available

name: Melatonin 10mg
notes: High dose; may be too much for most; studied for antioxidant and anti-cancer effects

name: 7-Keto DHEA

name: Desiccated Thyroid Glandular
notes: Over-the-counter glandular; contains thyroid hormones; consult healthcare provider

name: Adrenal Glandular
notes: Glandular support for adrenal fatigue; contains adrenal cofactors

name: Liver Glandular
notes: Concentrated whole food liver; rich in B12, folate, iron, CoQ10, and co-factors

name: CoQ10 Ubiquinone
notes: Essential for mitochondrial energy; statin depletion; oxidized form converted to ubiquinol

name: CoQ10 Ubiquinol
notes: Active, reduced form of CoQ10; better absorbed especially over age 40

name: PQQ
notes: Supports mitochondrial biogenesis and nerve growth factor; often paired with CoQ10

name: Alpha-Lipoic Acid
intakeConditions: on empty stomach for best absorption
notes: Universal antioxidant; regenerates other antioxidants; blood sugar and neuropathy support

name: R-Lipoic Acid
notes: More bioactive form than racemic ALA; superior to S-ALA; use stabilized form (Na-RALA)

name: Pterostilbene
notes: More bioavailable than resveratrol; SIRT1 activator; cognitive and cardiovascular interest

name: Fisetin
notes: Senolytic flavonoid; neuroprotective; highest amount in strawberries; longevity interest

name: Spermidine
notes: Induces autophagy; associated with longevity in epidemiological studies; found in wheat germ

name: Rapamycin
notes: PRESCRIPTION ONLY: mTOR inhibitor; longevity research interest; requires medical supervision

name: Metformin
intakeConditions: with food to reduce GI side effects
notes: PRESCRIPTION ONLY: diabetes drug used off-label for longevity; may deplete B12; AMPK activator

name: Lithium Orotate
notes: Trace mineral dose (not prescription lithium carbonate); neuroprotective interest; mood and brain support

name: NAD+ Precursor Blend
notes: Combination of NMN, NR, or other NAD+ precursors; longevity and mitochondrial support

name: Prenatal Vitamin
notes: Comprehensive prenatal formula; take throughout pregnancy and breastfeeding

name: Folate Prenatal Dose
notes: Higher dose folate for pregnancy; methylfolate form preferred for MTHFR variants; start before conception

name: Iron Prenatal
intakeConditions: with vitamin C; separate from calcium
notes: Daily recommended intake increases in pregnancy; check ferritin before supplementing

name: DHA Prenatal
notes: Critical for fetal brain and eye development; continue through breastfeeding

name: Choline
notes: Critical for fetal brain development; many prenatal vitamins lack adequate amounts; important for MTHFR

name: Postnatal Vitamin
notes: Optimized for postpartum recovery and breastfeeding; higher iron and DHA often included

name: Placenta Capsules
notes: Postpartum use; theoretical benefits for milk production, mood, and recovery; little clinical evidence

name: Electrolyte Powder
notes: Sodium, potassium, magnesium blend; no sugar; popular on low-carb diets

name: Sodium
notes: Often lacking on very low-carb or whole food diets; salt food to taste or add to water

name: Potassium Citrate
notes: Alkalizing potassium form; kidney stone prevention; 99mg OTC limit

name: AG1
intakeConditions: on empty stomach or with light meal; 8 oz water
notes: Comprehensive green powder with vitamins, adaptogens, probiotics; expensive but popular all-in-one

name: Spirulina
notes: Nutrient-dense algae; protein, B vitamins, iron, antioxidants; check for heavy metal testing

name: Chlorella
notes: Detox and nutrient support; broken cell wall form for better absorption; may bind heavy metals

name: Wheatgrass
notes: Chlorophyll-rich; alkalizing; trace minerals and enzymes; often grouped with greens powders

name: Moringa
notes: Nutrient-dense plant; protein, iron, calcium, antioxidants; anti-inflammatory properties

name: Niacin Flush Free
notes: Bound to inositol; marketed as no-flush; controversy over whether it has same benefits as niacin

name: Vitamin B Complex High Potency
notes: Higher potency B complex; expect bright yellow urine from riboflavin

name: NADH
notes: Active form of NAD; studied for chronic fatigue, Parkinson's, cognitive function

name: Alpha-GPC
notes: Highly bioavailable choline; crosses BBB; acetylcholine precursor; cognitive enhancement

name: CDP-Choline
notes: Choline + cytidine (uridine precursor); cognitive support; dopamine receptor support

name: Huperzine A
notes: AChE inhibitor; cognitive enhancement; cycle use to avoid tolerance; not daily long-term

name: Bacopa Monnieri
notes: Nootropic adaptogen; improves memory consolidation; takes 8-12 weeks for full effect

name: Lion's Mane Dual Extract

name: Phosphatidylserine
notes: Cortisol regulation and cognitive support; may help with HPA axis overactivation

name: Vinpocetine
notes: Cerebral blood flow support; nootropic; regulatory status varies by country

name: Astaxanthin
notes: Potent antioxidant carotenoid; skin, eye, and mitochondrial protection

name: Pycnogenol
notes: OPC antioxidant; vascular support, ADHD, endometriosis symptom interest

name: Meriva Curcumin
notes: Phytosome delivery for 29x better absorption than standard curcumin; no piperine needed

name: Colostrum
notes: Immune factors, growth factors, IgA; gut healing and immune modulation

name: Lactoferrin
notes: Iron-binding glycoprotein; antimicrobial, immune modulating, gut support

name: Digestive Bitters
notes: Stimulates digestive enzyme and HCl production; traditional digestive support

name: Activated Charcoal

name: Zeolite
notes: Silicate mineral for heavy metal and toxin binding; evidence limited; research ongoing

name: Diatomaceous Earth

name: Kelp

name: Methylated Multivitamin

name: Electrolyte Capsules
notes: Sodium-focused electrolyte capsules; useful for athletes and those on low-carb diets

name: Iodine Lugol's

name: Fulvic Acid
notes: Mineral transporter; supports nutrient absorption and mitochondrial function

name: Silica

name: MSM

notes: Allergy support (leaf); prostate and testosterone support (root); anti-inflammatory

name: Cat's Claw
notes: Immune modulating; used in Lyme co-infection protocols; anti-inflammatory

name: Andrographis
notes: Immune stimulating; antiviral; used for acute infections; bitter herb

name: Olive Leaf Extract

name: Pau d'Arco

name: Oregano Oil

name: Caprylic Acid
notes: Antifungal; candida protocol staple; medium-chain fatty acid

name: Monolaurin

name: Black Seed Oil

name: Elderflower
notes: Mild diuretic and antiviral; upper respiratory support; gentler than elderberry

name: Licorice Root

name: Marshmallow Root

name: Phosphatidylcholine

name: Vitamin K2 MK-7 High Dose

name: Pterostilbene + Resveratrol

name: Mangosteen Extract

name: Acai Berry Extract

name: Apigenin

name: Tributyrin

name: Pine Pollen

name: Tonkat Ali
notes: Supports testosterone, libido, and stress resilience; Physta standardized extract best studied

name: Fadogia Agrestis
notes: African herb used for testosterone support; limited human research; popular in biohacking community

name: Cistanche Tubulosa
